result:
Subject: About yesterday's payments outage

Yesterday, between [start time] and [end time] UTC, our payments service was unavailable for roughly four hours. During that window, some of you attempted transactions that failed outright or stalled mid-flow. We're sorry. If you were counting on us to move money and we didn't, that's on us.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule. Under a traffic spike, it evicted entries faster than we could repopulate them, which cascaded into timeouts across the payments path. It was our configuration, our blind spot, and our responsibility — not a third-party failure.

We've corrected the rule and redeployed. To prevent a repeat, we're moving all payments-path changes behind canary rollouts so a bad config hits a small slice of traffic first, and we're adding chaos testing in staging to stress cache and failover behavior under realistic load before anything ships to production.

As a goodwill credit, we're applying [$X / X%] to the accounts of every user with a failed transaction during the incident window — no action needed on your end; it will appear within [N] business days. If you ran into downstream issues we haven't caught, reply to this email and we'll make it right.

— [Name], [Title]
[Company]
